contents.title: IELTS English Podcast - Speaking Topic: Technology

vocabulary.definition: The subjects that are taught in a school, college, etc., or the things that are studied in a particular subject.
examples: The school has a new curriculum for science.
The curriculum includes history, geography, and mathematics.

vocabulary.definition: A person who is enthusiastic about new technology.
examples: He is a technophile and always buys the latest gadgets.
Technophiles are often early adopters of new technologies.
vocabulary.context_sentence: The first group are called technophiles.

vocabulary.definition: A person who dislikes or avoids new technology.
examples: My grandfather is a technophobe and refuses to use a computer.
Technophobes often find new technology intimidating.
vocabulary.context_sentence: The second group are called technophobes.

vocabulary.definition: A small, useful machine or device.
examples: She has a lot of gadgets in her kitchen.
This new gadget can translate languages in real time.
vocabulary.context_sentence: What gadgets do you use on a daily basis?

examples: I need to leave immediately.
The problem needs to be addressed immediately.
vocabulary.context_sentence: The ones that come to my mind immediately are my phone, my laptop,

vocabulary.definition: A portable electronic device designed for reading digital books.
examples: I prefer reading on my e-book reader.
E-book readers are convenient for travel.

vocabulary.definition: To improve something or make it more modern.
examples: I decided to upgrade my computer.
The company is upgrading its software.
vocabulary.context_sentence: but last year I decided to upgrade to kind of a top of the range.

vocabulary.definition: The process or art of making videos.
examples: He is studying videography at university.
Her videography skills are very impressive.
vocabulary.context_sentence: because as I said, I do like videography, particularly,

vocabulary.definition: No longer produced or used; out of date.
examples: The old technology has become obsolete.
Many traditional skills have become obsolete.
vocabulary.context_sentence: A lot of other technology became obsolete.

vocabulary.definition: A new idea, method, or product that completely changes the way that something is done.
examples: The invention of the internet was a game changer.
This new software is a game changer for our business.
vocabulary.context_sentence: I think the the biggest technological development that comes to my mind is the phone, the smart phone, because it was a real game changer.

vocabulary.definition: The state of depending on or trusting in something or someone.
examples: Our reliance on fossil fuels is causing environmental problems.
There is an increasing reliance on technology in education.
vocabulary.context_sentence: Another one that comes to my mind is our reliance on maps

vocabulary.definition: A thing that prevents someone from giving their attention to something else.
examples: The noise was a distraction.
I find that phones are a distraction.
vocabulary.context_sentence: I find that phones are a distraction.

grammar.title: Use of 'Whether...or'
grammar.explanation: Used to express alternatives or possibilities.
examples: Whether it rains or shines, we will go.
I don't know whether I should go or stay.
grammar.context_sentence: Whether it's work, I'm using my laptop, my phone

grammar.title: Use of 'on a daily basis'
grammar.explanation: To describe something that happens every day.
examples: I exercise on a daily basis.
She checks her email on a daily basis.
grammar.context_sentence: What gadgets do you use on a daily basis?

grammar.title: Use of 'I'd like to'
grammar.explanation: A polite way of saying 'I want to'.
examples: I'd like to go to the cinema.
I'd like to ask a question.
grammar.context_sentence: I'd like to tell you about my phone.

grammar.title: Use of 'because' and 'because of'
grammar.explanation: To explain the reason for something. 'Because' is followed by a clause, 'because of' by a noun phrase.
grammar.structure: Because + clause; Because of + noun phrase
examples: I stayed home because I was sick.
I stayed home because of my illness.
grammar.context_sentence: because as I said, I do like videography, particularly,

grammar.title: Use of 'in my opinion'
examples: In my opinion, this is the best solution.
In my opinion, the movie was very good.
grammar.context_sentence: In my opinion, it's definitely the best phone I've ever had,

grammar.title: Use of 'For example'
examples: I enjoy many sports, for example, football and basketball.
There are many different types of fruit, for example, apples and oranges.
grammar.context_sentence: For example, people rely on modern technology for writing

grammar.title: Use of 'I think'
grammar.explanation: To express an opinion or belief.
examples: I think it's a good idea.
I think we should go now.
grammar.context_sentence: I think the the biggest technological development that comes to my mind is the phone, the smart phone,
